message:
feat(trace): add DPOR race detection, TLA+ export, and schedule explorer

Introduce three new subsystems completing the Phase 5 DPOR infrastructure:

- src/trace/dpor.rs (303 lines): Race detection engine that identifies
  happens-before violations between concurrent trace events. Produces
  backtrack points for systematic schedule exploration. Races are detected
  between events that access the same region/task and are not ordered by
  the happens-before relation.

- src/trace/tla_export.rs (573 lines): TLA+ specification exporter that
  translates runtime traces into formal TLA+ models. Supports region
  hierarchies, task lifecycle states, budget constraints, and temporal
  properties. Enables model checking of scheduler invariants against
  concrete execution traces.

- src/lab/explorer.rs (455 lines): DPOR-guided schedule explorer that
  systematically varies task interleaving seeds to discover distinct
  equivalence classes. Tracks coverage metrics (discovery rate, class
  run counts) and detects invariant violations across schedules. Uses
  Foata canonicalization fingerprints to identify equivalent executions.

- tests/dpor_exploration.rs (374 lines): End-to-end integration tests
  covering the independence relation, Foata canonicalization, race
  detection, schedule exploration, coverage metrics, and the full
  trace → canonicalize → race-detect pipeline.

- src/lab/mod.rs, src/trace/mod.rs: Module declarations and re-exports
  for the new dpor, tla_export, and explorer modules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
